content.text:
# Run the project's mandatory finalisation checklist before reporting any task as done

## Steps

1. Run `npm run typecheck` and capture all output.
2. Run `npm run lint` and capture all output.
3. If either produced any errors or warnings:
   - For lint issues — run `npm run lint:fix` first to auto-fix what it can, then re-run `npm run lint` to see what remains.
   - Fix **every** remaining issue — do not skip, suppress, or dismiss any output.
   - If you are unsure whether a problem existed before your changes, run `git stash`, repeat both commands to establish a pre-change baseline, then `git stash pop` and fix only the new issues.
   - After fixing, re-run both commands to confirm they are clean.
4. Repeat until both commands exit with code 0 and report no errors or warnings.

## Done criterion

`npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` both exit with code 0 and report no errors or warnings. Only then report the task as complete.

## Notes

- Fix issues in the order they appear — later errors are often caused by earlier ones.
- Avoid silencing a lint rule or adding a `@ts-ignore` / `eslint-disable` comment to make the output go away — fix the underlying problem. If a suppression is truly necessary (false positive, required vendor API), scope it as narrowly as possible (single line, specific rule) and add a short inline comment explaining why.
- If a typecheck error is in a generated file (`prisma/generated/`, `prisma/zod/`), do not edit it — regenerate it with `npx prisma generate` instead.

content.text:
Typecheck, lint, and the component tests all pass clean.

In [PlayerArse.tsx](src/components/PlayerArse/PlayerArse.tsx#L13-L16) I made the ARSE tooltip keyboard/touch accessible:
- Added `tabIndex={0}` to the `Title` so it can receive keyboard focus.
- Enabled `events={{ hover: true, focus: true, touch: true }}` on the `Tooltip` — Mantine's default disables focus and touch triggers, so previously the tooltip only ever showed on mouse hover.

Mantine's `useRole` (via Floating UI) automatically wires up `aria-describedby` when the tooltip opens, so screen reader users get the description too once it's reachable by focus.

Defining --profile-overlay-opacity on :global(:root) introduces a global side effect from a CSS module and can create cross-component coupling. Prefer scoping the variable to a local wrapper class (e.g., .statsRow or .identity) or driving this via the theme so it only affects the PlayerProfile surface.

content.text:
All 19 tests pass, typecheck and lint are clean.

Replaced the obsolete `summary` attribute on the Info table in [PlayerProfile.tsx](src/components/PlayerProfile/PlayerProfile.tsx#L107-L113) with a proper `aria-labelledby` linked to the "Info" heading via `useId()` — a more reliably-supported accessible name than the deprecated HTML5 `summary` attribute, and avoids duplicating the "Info" text as a separate string.

I have this comment from code review: would it be better to pass in the title order, or to move the title and divider into the parent and just render the table in such components?

This promotes the card heading to an H2 (via Mantine Title order={2}). On PlayerProfile, there is already an H1 (player name) and section H2s (e.g., Info/History), so these stat cards should generally remain H3 to preserve a logical heading hierarchy for screen readers and document outline. Consider reverting to order={3}, or make the heading level configurable via a prop so the parent can choose the appropriate order.

content.text:
They're only ever used from `PlayerProfile.tsx`, so this is a real either/or rather than a reusability concern.

I'd pass the title order in as a prop (default `3`) rather than hoisting `Title`+`Divider` into the parent. Each card keeps its self-contained "Paper with heading + divider + table" shape, which matches how `PlayerArse` already works and keeps their Storybook stories/tests rendering a complete, meaningful unit in isolation. Hoisting the heading to the parent would remove the small duplication (three `Title`/`Divider` pairs), but it means `PlayerProfile` has to know each card's internal layout details and reconstruct the same wrapper markup three times at the call site — that's arguably more coupling, not less, for a single-use-site component. The prop is a one-line change per component and directly fixes the heading-hierarchy bug.
OK, do that.

content.text:
All 27 tests pass, typecheck and lint are clean.

Added an optional `titleOrder?: TitleOrder` prop (default `3`) to `PlayerResults`, `PlayerPositions`, and `PlayerArse`, each now rendering their card heading at `order={titleOrder}` instead of the hardcoded `2`. This drops their default heading level to H3, keeping `PlayerProfile`'s H1 (player name) → H2 (Info/History) → H3 (stat cards) hierarchy correct, while leaving the level open for the parent to override if ever needed.

Adding tabIndex={0} to a heading makes it an extra keyboard tab stop, which can be noisy for keyboard and assistive-tech users. Prefer making a non-heading wrapper focusable (or using a dedicated help/info control) and ensure the tooltip content is announced (e.g., via aria-describedby/aria-label) rather than forcing focus onto the heading element.

All 22 tests pass, typecheck and lint clean.

In [PlayerArse.tsx](src/components/PlayerArse/PlayerArse.tsx#L14-L24), the "ARSE" heading is no longer the tooltip target — it's back to a plain, non-focusable heading.
